task_completion.description: Import the survey data CSV into TopoCal
task_completion.visual_evidence: TopoCal interface showing the imported survey points (a grid or cloud of points) or an import dialog actively loading 'gilpin_survey.csv'.

task_completion.description: Generate a terrain model (DTM) from the imported points
task_completion.visual_evidence: TopoCal interface displaying a triangulated irregular network (TIN) or 3D surface model generated from the survey points.

task_completion.description: Generate contour lines based on the terrain model and specifications
task_completion.visual_evidence: TopoCal interface showing topographic contour lines drawn across the terrain model (should reflect 1m intermediate and 5m index intervals).

task_completion.description: Export the contour map as a DXF file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save/export dialog in TopoCal or a file explorer window showing 'contour_map.dxf' successfully created in 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\GilpinProject\'.

task_completion.description: Create the slope analysis report text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., Notepad) open and saving a file named 'slope_analysis.txt' in the 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\GilpinProject\' directory.

task_completion.description: Include all required analysis elements in the slope report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor displaying the report contents, which must explicitly include: elevation range, contour line counts, identification of steep gradient zones, and the recommendation that slopes exceeding 30% are unsuitable.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the TopoCal GUI to process the data
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show active interaction with the TopoCal application windows, menus, and toolbars, rather than bypassing the software using terminal commands or Python scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the DXF was genuinely exported from TopoCal
integrity.visual_evidence: The DXF file should be seen being generated via TopoCal's export or save functionality, not copied, moved, or downloaded from an external source.

integrity.description: Verify the report was manually constructed based on analysis
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen writing or pasting the report content into a text editor based on observations from TopoCal, rather than executing a script to auto-generate the text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The survey data contains exactly 200 points in UTM Zone 13N. Contour intervals must be 1m (intermediate) and 5m (index). The exported DXF must contain at least 20 LWPOLYLINE entities (representing the contours). The slope analysis report must contain numeric gradient references and explicitly state that slopes exceeding 30% are unsuitable for development.
